name: Tito's Handmade Vodka
notes: clean, black pepper, sweet corn, citrus peel, mineral, smooth
research: 2025 #1 spirit by volume in multiple US control-state markets (Royal Batch / retail data).
best: Vodka soda, martini, member wants America's top-selling spirit name.
avoid: User wants brown spirits or heavy flavor only.
why: Recommend when the member names Tito's or wants the best-selling US vodka — clean, neutral, cocktail-ready.
blurb: Tito's Handmade — America's best-selling vodka by volume. Clean corn spirit built for soda, tonic, and letting a mild cigar speak.

name: Smirnoff No. 21 Vodka
notes: neutral, light citrus, grain, mineral, smooth, faint sweetness
research: Top value-tier vodka contributor in 2025 US sales (Royal Batch).
best: Budget vodka cocktails, party pours, flavored-margarita base.
avoid: User wants premium wheat vodka character only.
why: Recommend for value vodka cocktails — neutral base that will not fight a Connecticut cigar.
blurb: Smirnoff No. 21 — the value vodka America still buys in volume. Neutral, mixable, and honest about what it is.

name: New Amsterdam Vodka
notes: citrus, clean grain, light sweetness, mineral, smooth, faint vanilla
research: Major value/flavored vodka segment performer in 2025 (Royal Batch).
best: House vodka, citrus cocktails, budget-conscious member.
why: Recommend when the lounge needs an approachable house vodka — citrus lift without Grey Goose pricing.
blurb: New Amsterdam — citrus-leaning grain vodka in the value tier America actually buys. Fine for highballs beside a mild smoke.

name: Lunazul Blanco Tequila
notes: cooked agave, citrus, white pepper, mineral, light herbal, clean finish
research: Strong accessible tequila performer in 2025 US sales (Royal Batch).
best: Tequila beginner, margarita night, authentic agave without premium price.
avoid: User wants only luxury añejo sweetness.
why: Recommend when the member wants real blanco agave on a budget — pepper, citrus, and cooked agave.
blurb: Lunazul Blanco — 100% agave authenticity without the premium sticker. The tequila Americans bought when they were not splurging on Patrón.

name: Don Julio Reposado
notes: cooked agave, vanilla, caramel, oak, light pepper, honey, dried fruit
research: Repeat-buyer reposado in 2025 premium tequila segment (Royal Batch).
best: Member moving from cocktails to sipping tequila, medium-full cigar night.
avoid: User wants only blanco crispness or ultra-luxury 1942.
why: Recommend when the member wants Don Julio character below 1942 — vanilla, oak, and agave for sipping.
blurb: Don Julio Reposado — the tequila people buy twice. Vanilla and oak over cooked agave when someone is done with shots and ready to sip.

name: Jack Daniel's Old No. 7
notes: banana, caramel, vanilla, toasted oak, light smoke, brown sugar
research: Among most-purchased American spirits nationwide in 2025 (Royal Batch).
best: Jack Daniel's name ask, Tennessee whiskey curious, classic bar call.
avoid: User wants Kentucky bourbon or Scotch only.
why: Recommend when the member wants the iconic Jack pour — banana bread sweetness and charcoal-mellowed Tennessee oak.
blurb: Jack Daniel's Old No. 7 — the black-label Tennessee whiskey America never stopped buying. Banana, caramel, and charcoal mellow beside a Habano.

name: Jim Beam Kentucky Straight Bourbon
notes: vanilla, caramel, oak, light corn sweetness, gentle spice, toasted nuts
research: Household bourbon staple in 2025 American whiskey sales (Royal Batch).
best: Bourbon beginner, Old Fashioned base, budget Kentucky pour.
avoid: User wants allocated or high-proof only.
why: Recommend as America's everyday bourbon — vanilla, caramel, and oak without pretense.
blurb: Jim Beam White Label — the bourbon in every American pantry. Vanilla and caramel Kentucky honesty beside a medium cigar.

name: Maker's Mark Kentucky Straight Bourbon
notes: vanilla, caramel, honey, orange peel, oak, baking spice, almond
research: Premium bourbon steady seller for gifting and sipping in 2025 (Royal Batch).
best: Wheated bourbon fan, gift pour, red-wax recognition, medium cigar.
avoid: User wants high-rye spice bomb only.
why: Recommend when the member wants wheated sweetness with red-wax credibility — honey, vanilla, and soft spice.
blurb: Maker's Mark — wheated bourbon with the red wax everyone recognizes. Honey and vanilla over orange peel when rye feels too sharp.

name: Jameson Irish Whiskey
notes: honey, green apple, vanilla, light spice, toasted wood, floral malt
research: Top-selling imported whiskey style in US 2025 rankings (Royal Batch).
best: Irish whiskey beginner, St. Patrick's crowd, green-bottle recognition.
avoid: User wants single malt Scotch only.
why: Recommend when the member wants the Jameson everyone knows — honey, apple, and easy Irish malt.
blurb: Jameson Original — Ireland's green bottle on every American bar. Honey and green apple malt that works neat, on ice, or beside a medium cigar.

name: Hennessy VS
notes: grape, oak, vanilla, almond, light spice, dried fruit, honey
research: Top cognac by dollar sales in US 2025 (Royal Batch); VS entry tier.
best: Hennessy name at VS tier, cognac-curious, hip-hop/culture recognition.
why: Recommend when Hennessy is the ask at entry tier — grape, almond, and oak before VSOP pricing.
blurb: Hennessy VS — the cognac tier America buys in volume. Grape and almond over French oak when the member wants the Hennessy name without XO money.
